items.title: Crystal - Send a reboot signal to the kernel (or other signals)
items.author: Fulgurance
items.content:
Hi guys, I have a question, on a linux system, with , how can I send the reboot signal to the linux kernel to perform a reboot ?

Basically I have the project to write an init system. I would like to understand how a reboot work.

Is it basically first the init system close all process properly and then write some value in to ask for a reboot?

I am asking for reboot, but as well for other request , like a

If yes, can I have an example please?

items.title: How to generate methods of module for sub class via macro?
items.author: aiac

items.title: How to : class specifier argument for method?
items.author: AlexR
items.content:
In my class, I’m using an Array to store union type elements : @items = Array(A | B | C).new.

How should I declare a method that takes a class specifier argument (either of class A, B, or C) and returns the next array element of that class? The invocation would be akin to found = my_instance.get_next_item(C) .

I’ve tried something like def get_next_item(k : Class) : k ... but am getting a Error: unexpected token: "k".

If OTOH I declare the method thus : def get_next(k : Class) : A | B | C, I get a compilation error at the first invokation of : Error: unexpected token: "k"

items.title: Meld - A CLI tool to manage shards
items.author: cmittal790
items.content:
My name is Chetan Mittal, and I have been a Ruby and Rails developer since 2005. Just a few days back I started gaining interest in Crsytal Lang after I started using LavinMQ message broker.

While learning how to program in Crystal I found out that it lacked an easy tool like Bundler is to Ruby, and Cargo is to Rust. So I started developing Meld.

Currently, it still a work in progress, but you can:-

$ crystal run src/meld.cr -- help

Meld - Crystal Lang Package Manager

  meld [options] <command> [command options]

  init                               Initialize project (shard.yml)

  add <shard> [-v|-b|-t|-c] [--dev]  Add a shard (pin with version/branch/tag/commit)

  remove <shard>                     Remove a shard from shard.yml and lib/

  search <query>                     Search for available shards

  install                            Install dependencies

  update [shard]                     Update all or a specific shard

  exec <cmd>                         Run a command in project context

  binstubs <shard>                   Generate executable wrappers

  global install <shard> [...]       Build and install shard executables globally

  help [command]                     Show help (global or for a command)

    -v, --version                    Show version

    -h, --help                       Show help

The basic features like meld add <shard_name>, remove, install, etc work except a few such as creating binstubs. Also, it can build and install a package such as LavinMQ globally without using make.

items.title: Language Popularity: Try to negotiate with JetBrains to support the Crystal language in their IDE
items.author: Sega100500
items.content:
It’s a very popular JetBrains IDEs ( JetBrains IDEs: enjoy an exceptional developer experience ) for programming. Unfortunately, it doesn’t yet support the Crystal language. Try to negotiate with JetBrains at least a plugin for it that supports Crystal syntax. Believe me, this could greatly contribute to Crystal’s popularity. I currently use RubyMine for writing programs on Crystal, but the syntax is still slightly different.

12 posts - 7 participants

items.title: Crystal Newsletter Subscribe not working on https://crystal-lang.org/newsletter/
items.author: Sega100500
items.content:
I’m a newbie (though I’ve been using Ruby since 2011). But I’m frustrated by the fact that some things don’t work on the language’s official website https://crystal-lang.org/. How can you expect a language to be popular if something doesn’t even work on your official website?

It’s important to understand that marketing is also important for programming languages. Everything should work on the official website, but instead I get the following message:

———

We couldn’t process your request at this time. Please try again later. If you are seeing this message repeatedly, please contact Support with the following information:

date: Wed Sep 24 2025 14:50:49 GMT+0500 (Екатеринбург, стандартное время)

user agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:143.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/143.0

We’re sorry for the inconvenience and appreciate your patience while we get everything straightened out. ———

But despite this minor annoyance …

I’m delighted with Crystal language! Thank you for creating such a wonderful language! I’ll keep trying to learn and use it. Thank you!
